rationale: Each ensures clause pins a projection of post_self_ to the corresponding pre-state value; transitively post1 and post2 agree on every observable projection. Previous attempt's assert foralls had auto-chosen low-confidence triggers (e.g. `pre.thread_dom().contains(t_ptr)`, `post1.endpoint_dom().contains(e_ptr)`) that didn't fire when `det_<f>_equal` probed `post1.get_thread(t_ptr)` / `post1.get_endpoint(e_ptr)`. The fix is explicit `#![trigger post1_self_.get_X(p)]` and `#![trigger post2_self_.get_X(p)]` annotations so the introduced foralls match `det_<f>_equal`'s instantiations, plus inlining `pre.get_endpoint(endpoint_ptr).queue@[0]` instead of a `let` binding to avoid any unfolding ambiguity.
